She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.\\n\\nGiven that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.\\n\\nNo substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJames\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:43:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

[{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

content.text: Downloads returned "version not found" (seeded metadata-only docs); titles are the scope signal. Let me extract the AP queue detail from the saved file and pull AR/WIP transaction-level data.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/820922ee-26bf-4d1a-9616-3983dbbed4fa/tool-results/
f=$(ls -t mcp-mcp_fba99f21-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-*.txt | head -1)
echo "FILE: $f"
python3 -c "
import json,sys
d=json.load(open('$f'))
inner=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=inner.get('ap_invoices',[])
print('total_ap_invoices', inner.get('total_ap_invoices'), 'returned', len(inv))
from datetime import date
today=date(2026,6,12)
rows=[]
for i in inv:
    st=i.get('status')
    rows.append(i)
# pending only
pend=[i for i in inv if i.get('status')=='pending_approval']
print('pending_approval count:', len(pend))
def age(d):
    try:
        y,m,dd=map(int,d[:10].split('-'));return (today-date(y,m,dd)).days
    except: return None
for i in sorted(pend, key=lambda x:x.get('invoice_date') or ''):
    print('---')
    print('id',i.get('id'),'ven',i.get('vendor_invoice_number') or i.get('invoice_number'),'vendor',i.get('vendor_name'))
    print(' amt',i.get('amount'),i.get('currency'),'gl',i.get('gl_account_number'),'status',i.get('status'))
    print(' inv_date',i.get('invoice_date'),'recv',i.get('time_received'),'age_days',age(i.get('invoice_date') or ''))
    print(' memo',(i.get('memo') or i.get('description') or '')[:120])
"

content.text: All 87 Northstar AP invoices are in `pending_approval` — none approved or paid. Let me compute totals and aging buckets, and read the JE list for WIP/billing tie‑out.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/820922ee-26bf-4d1a-9616-3983dbbed4fa/tool-results/
f=$(ls -t mcp-mcp_fba99f21-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-*.txt | head -1)
python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('$f')); data=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=data.get('journal_entries') or data.get('entries') or data
print('keys:', list(data.keys()) if isinstance(data,dict) else type(data))
print('total JEs FP-2026-04:', data.get('total_journal_entries'), 'returned', len(jes) if isinstance(jes,list) else '?')
for je in jes:
    accts=[(l.get('account_number'),l.get('debit'),l.get('credit')) for l in je.get('lines',[])]
    desc=je.get('description','')
    print('::',je.get('id'),'|',je.get('status'),'|',je.get('entry_type'),'|',desc[:60])
    for a,dr,cr in accts:
        print('     ',a,'DR',dr,'CR',cr)
" 2>&1 | head -120

content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"3d6be41bc0a14f8d926f334405b27f9e\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"lucia.ferreira@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Multi-entity client win: assignment and setup\", \"content\": \"Hello Lucia,\\n\\nPlease create the client setup record in Airtable today and open the initial Linear workstreams for accounting, tax, and compliance. George should be included on the accounting side once the intake data is live.\\n\\nMaintain strict version control on the onboarding pack and keep the first-phase scope limited to the signed engagement letters.\\n\\nRegards,\\nSteven\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"e66c4ac7dbd54c308c5bbc2965e6515a\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-22T14:35:24Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"0655a4d74286450994695e108f23aef0\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Multi-entity client win: initial commercial view\", \"content\": \"Hi Matthew,\\n\\nConfirmed. The engagement letters are executed for the multi-entity client, and final accountability remains with us.\\n\\nWe will open on a controlled basis, define scope tightly, and protect quality from the outset. Please align your team only after the entity inventory and service boundaries are clear.\\n\\nRegards,\\nSteven\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"6d015496e51547afbbb8dff1e467a465\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-22T14:09:51Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"6d015496e51547afbbb8dff1e467a465\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Multi-entity client win: initial commercial view\", \"content\": \"Hi Steven,\\n\\nThank you for confirming the signed engagement letters. This is a strong win for the firm, and it is encouraging that the opportunity spans accounting, tax, and compliance rather than a single-point service line.\\n\\nFrom an accounting services perspective, I would recommend that we open the engagement conservatively and avoid any implied expansion of scope before the entity structure, reporting cadence, and prior-period cleanup items are fully mapped. If you are comfortable, I will coordinate the initial service assumptions with the team once we have the onboarding package.\\n\\nBest, \\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-22T14:06:40Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nI reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.\\n\\nOnce Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.\\n\\nThis should give you the approval chain you need before filing.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-15T19:48:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"8aa0683646a74f7a8f5be6be60a6f8c9\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker check: bank feed and recon request wording for Monday onboarding\", \"content\": \"Hi Edith,\\n\\nThanks for the kickoff draft. The request list is very close, but I have one question on the final wording before we issue it to the client: do you want the bank authorization form referenced alongside the engagement letter, or called out in a separate line item? Once I have that, I can tighten the language and get it ready to send.\\n\\nRegards,\\nJones\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"95587ea5411846f6a87acb63a07a908f\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-13T15:12:17Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"638022acb57e4199967c78fd2be0263c\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send\", \"content\": \"Hi Sean,\\n\\nThank you for the update. That is enough for now, and I agree the engagement letter should remain the only item flagged separately at this stage. Please keep the remaining documents grouped in the tracker so we can finish triaging the blockers before sending anything else to the client.\\n\\nBest,\\nEdith\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"95f7fa04dd5a414295e35583ea680686\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-30T15:17:42Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"95f7fa04dd5a414295e35583ea680686\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send\", \"content\": \"Hi Edith,\\n\\nI reviewed the tracker after Blue's updates and the open items still look limited to the engagement letter, prior-year trial balance, bank authorization form, reporting cadence, and the remaining onboarding documents. Before I send the client note, can you confirm whether you want us to hold it until the bank authorization form is in hand, or should we send the current list now and flag that item separately?\\n\\nBest,\\nSean\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"4fc0b0f5f69a4dc294da46281b88aa11\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-30T15:10:16Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"248a3fa1eb3d400da463f5016435f826\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"mateo.kovac@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"nia.okafor@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI have finished the intake packet review against the Monday checklist, and we are still missing the signed engagement letter plus the prior-year trial balance. Those are holding the client request from going out cleanly. Since this has already come up in the last two follow-ups, can you please loop in your manager so we can get a decision on the missing items and move this forward today?\\n\\nBest,\\nMateo\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"5fc465b103884519901c5e4942cb70eb\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-30T14:39:34Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"5d38b8bf72ee44f2a9cda640f81aeb6e\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Standard AP workflow for managed clients\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nI wanted to reopen the AP workflow standardization discussion for our managed clients. We are still seeing variation in how invoices are routed, what backup is required, and when items move to approval. That is creating avoidable follow-up and making it harder to keep client files consistent.\\n\\nMy recommendation is that we set one baseline process for all managed clients, with limited exceptions only where the engagement letter or client policy requires it. At a minimum, the standard should cover invoice intake, coding support, approval routing, documentation of exceptions, and final storage of the approved package.\\n\\nCan you pull together the current AP handling approach you are seeing across your clients and flag where the process is breaking down most often? If helpful, include a few examples of invoices that have been stalled so we can use those to define the workflow.\\n\\nThanks,\\nEdith Banda\\nAccounts Senior\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-19T18:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"1a98e3cbb21d4f83bcbad7dcf3ef50fb\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"mateo.kovac@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Monday kickoff: final document request checks before client send\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nFollowing up on the kickoff review, the signed engagement letter came in this morning, but the ownership schedule and prior-year trial balance are still outstanding. I am comparing the intake packet against the checklist now, and I do not see any authority or consent gaps beyond those two missing items. I will send a status update as soon as I finish the blocker review.\\n\\nBest,\\nMargot\", \"cc\": [\"marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"ad74f09aed624e2f8559076046f24b14\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-16T15:14:36Z\", \"is_read\": false}]"}

[{"email_id": "3d6be41bc0a14f8d926f334405b27f9e", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["lucia.ferreira@brookfieldcpas.com", "george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Multi-entity client win: assignment and setup", "content": "Hello Lucia,

Please create the client setup record in Airtable today and open the initial Linear workstreams for accounting, tax, and compliance. George should be included on the accounting side once the intake data is live.

Maintain strict version control on the onboarding pack and keep the first-phase scope limited to the signed engagement letters.

Regards,
Steven", "cc": [], "parent_id": "e66c4ac7dbd54c308c5bbc2965e6515a", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-22T14:35:24Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "0655a4d74286450994695e108f23aef0", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Multi-entity client win: initial commercial view", "content": "Hi Matthew,

Confirmed. The engagement letters are executed for the multi-entity client, and final accountability remains with us.

We will open on a controlled basis, define scope tightly, and protect quality from the outset. Please align your team only after the entity inventory and service boundaries are clear.

Regards,
Steven", "cc": [], "parent_id": "6d015496e51547afbbb8dff1e467a465", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-22T14:09:51Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "6d015496e51547afbbb8dff1e467a465", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Multi-entity client win: initial commercial view", "content": "Hi Steven,

Thank you for confirming the signed engagement letters. This is a strong win for the firm, and it is encouraging that the opportunity spans accounting, tax, and compliance rather than a single-point service line.

From an accounting services perspective, I would recommend that we open the engagement conservatively and avoid any implied expansion of scope before the entity structure, reporting cadence, and prior-period cleanup items are fully mapped. If you are comfortable, I will coordinate the initial service assumptions with the team once we have the onboarding package.

Best, 
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-22T14:06:40Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

I reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.

Once Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.

This should give you the approval chain you need before filing.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T19:48:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "8aa0683646a74f7a8f5be6be60a6f8c9", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Blocker check: bank feed and recon request wording for Monday onboarding", "content": "Hi Edith,

Thanks for the kickoff draft. The request list is very close, but I have one question on the final wording before we issue it to the client: do you want the bank authorization form referenced alongside the engagement letter, or called out in a separate line item? Once I have that, I can tighten the language and get it ready to send.

Regards,
Jones", "cc": [], "parent_id": "95587ea5411846f6a87acb63a07a908f", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-13T15:12:17Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "638022acb57e4199967c78fd2be0263c", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com", "blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send", "content": "Hi Sean,

Thank you for the update. That is enough for now, and I agree the engagement letter should remain the only item flagged separately at this stage. Please keep the remaining documents grouped in the tracker so we can finish triaging the blockers before sending anything else to the client.

Best,
Edith", "cc": [], "parent_id": "95f7fa04dd5a414295e35583ea680686", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-30T15:17:42Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "95f7fa04dd5a414295e35583ea680686", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com", "blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send", "content": "Hi Edith,

I reviewed the tracker after Blue's updates and the open items still look limited to the engagement letter, prior-year trial balance, bank authorization form, reporting cadence, and the remaining onboarding documents. Before I send the client note, can you confirm whether you want us to hold it until the bank authorization form is in hand, or should we send the current list now and flag that item separately?

Best,
Sean", "cc": [], "parent_id": "4fc0b0f5f69a4dc294da46281b88aa11", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-30T15:10:16Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "248a3fa1eb3d400da463f5016435f826", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "mateo.kovac@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com", "nia.okafor@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Monday onboarding: final gaps and revised doc list for client send", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I have finished the intake packet review against the Monday checklist, and we are still missing the signed engagement letter plus the prior-year trial balance. Those are holding the client request from going out cleanly. Since this has already come up in the last two follow-ups, can you please loop in your manager so we can get a decision on the missing items and move this forward today?

Best,
Mateo", "cc": [], "parent_id": "5fc465b103884519901c5e4942cb70eb", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-30T14:39:34Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "5d38b8bf72ee44f2a9cda640f81aeb6e", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Standard AP workflow for managed clients", "content": "Hi Emily,

I wanted to reopen the AP workflow standardization discussion for our managed clients. We are still seeing variation in how invoices are routed, what backup is required, and when items move to approval. That is creating avoidable follow-up and making it harder to keep client files consistent.

My recommendation is that we set one baseline process for all managed clients, with limited exceptions only where the engagement letter or client policy requires it. At a minimum, the standard should cover invoice intake, coding support, approval routing, documentation of exceptions, and final storage of the approved package.

Can you pull together the current AP handling approach you are seeing across your clients and flag where the process is breaking down most often? If helpful, include a few examples of invoices that have been stalled so we can use those to define the workflow.

Thanks,
Edith Banda
Accounts Senior", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-19T18:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "1a98e3cbb21d4f83bcbad7dcf3ef50fb", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "mateo.kovac@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Monday kickoff: final document request checks before client send", "content": "Hi Daniel,

Following up on the kickoff review, the signed engagement letter came in this morning, but the ownership schedule and prior-year trial balance are still outstanding. I am comparing the intake packet against the checklist now, and I do not see any authority or consent gaps beyond those two missing items. I will send a status update as soon as I finish the blocker review.

Best,
Margot", "cc": ["marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "ad74f09aed624e2f8559076046f24b14", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-16T15:14:36Z", "is_read": false}]

input.content:
# Northstar Legal — Engagement Status, Scope & Billing Review

**Prepared for:** Daniel Jones (Accounts Manager) — ahead of partner discussion with Matthew Li
**Prepared by:** George McAdam (Accounts Senior)
**Date:** 12 June 2026
**Entity:** northstar_legal | **Classification:** Restricted | **Retention:** FIRM_INTERNAL

---

## 1. Bottom line

The Northstar engagement looks "messy from a few angles" because three things are true at once:

1. **We are performing accounting, annual-accounts, tax-compliance and tax-advisory work for Northstar, but the only engagement letter on file in Records Vault is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026‑05‑11).** There is no bookkeeping / management-accounts / tax / advisory engagement letter or change-order/addendum on file. The expanded work is therefore **undocumented as to scope and fee** — the opposite of how the parallel Acme Cloud overage was handled (documented Engagement Letter Addendum #1, partner-countersigned).
2. **The WIP we are carrying does not tie out.** WIP (acct 119000) is **$2,013,942.79** and only ever grows — it is recognised straight to revenue and is **never relieved to AR/billings**. There is no WIP→invoice conversion happening.
3. **The AP queue we run for Northstar is badly backed up** — **all 87** vendor invoices sit in `pending_approval` (**$302,063.22**), 75 of them (**$274,917**) over 90 days, none approved or paid. The comms only ever discuss 3 small CrownPeak items (~$1.8K). The story in Slack/email does **not** match what the numbers show.

A scope conversation (absorb vs. change order) is warranted. Supporting detail below.

---

## 2. Accounts Receivable — outstanding, aging & stale risk

GL is Northstar's own ledger, which we maintain.

| Metric | Account | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| Accounts Receivable – Client Billings | 110000 | **$647,359.49** |
| Allowance for Doubtful Accounts | 117000 | **$1,057,727.96** (credit) |
| Bad Debt Expense (YTD) | 590000 | **$427,018.64** |
| Deferred Revenue – Client Retainers | 240000 | $2,043,052.32 |

**Stale-risk read:**
- The allowance ($1.06M) is **~163% of net AR** and bad-debt expense is **$427K YTD** — both abnormally high. In FP‑2026‑04 alone there were **~$49.9K of bad-debt write-offs** across five separate JEs (e.g. $16,033.85, $15,924.32, $8,421.99, $7,258.47, $2,274.39).
- This pattern — a reserve larger than the live ledger plus continuous monthly write-offs — is the signal that **aged receivables are routinely going uncollectible and being absorbed through bad debt** rather than collected. That is the "going stale" risk in concrete terms.
- Cash is still turning (client-payment JEs of $133.2K, $106.1K, $82.9K in the month), so this is a **collectibility / aged-bucket** problem, not a total-collection failure.
- *Limitation:* invoice-level aging is not exposed in the GL; the reserve and write-off behaviour is the available stale-risk evidence. Recommend a true AR aging be pulled from the billing sub-ledger before the partner meeting if Matthew wants bucketed figures.

---

## 3. Work in Process — does it tie to what's invoiced/approved? **No.**

| Account | Mar‑26 close | Apr‑26 close | Current |
|---|---|---|---|
| WIP – Unbilled Time (119000) | $1,804,471.51 | $1,914,360.46 | **$2,013,942.79** |

- WIP recognised in FP‑2026‑04: **~$69.1K posted** (JEs $3,582.87 + $38,556.46 + $26,926.85) **plus $46,884.36 approved-but-not-posted** (je_067b4a22281e4459). Matches the BD1 Slack note ("WIP $69.0K driven by Hartmann discovery and Wexler appellate").
- **The structural problem:** WIP recognition entries are booked **DR 119000 / CR Revenue** (401000/404000/406000). Billing entries are booked separately as **DR 110000 AR / CR Revenue** ("Litigation engagement revenue realization"). **Nothing ever credits 119000.** Across every period reviewed, WIP has *zero* credit activity.
- Net effect: **WIP is not relieved when work is billed**, it accumulates indefinitely, and **it does not tie to billings/AR or to approved invoices.** Either WIP is being recognised to revenue prematurely (and never converted to a bill) or the billing path is mis-booked. Either way, **$2.0M of carried WIP is unsupported by a corresponding billing/relief mechanism.**
- Several FP‑2026‑04 entries are **approved but never posted** in a now-closed/locked period (WIP $46,884.36; marketing $15,022.81; IOLTA withdrawal $46,735.90; CLE $2,706.31; bad debt $2,274.39) — a close-hygiene issue that compounds the tie-out gap.

---

## 4. Scope vs. engagement letter — have we gone past what's covered?

**On file in Records Vault (engagement_letter kind, northstar_legal):** exactly **one** document —
*"Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter — Executed 2026‑05‑11"* (restricted, FIRM_INTERNAL).

**Work we have actually been performing for Northstar (evidenced in GL/JEs and Vault docs):**
- Monthly **bookkeeping & close** (continuous JE prep/posting by Brookfield staff, July 2025 → present).
- **Management accounts** packages (e.g. FP‑2026‑04 Management Accounts Package, filed 2026‑05‑07).
- **FY2025 annual accounts preparation** — working file + IOLTA/operating cash recs, fixed-asset rollforward, prepaid schedule; ASC‑710 partner-draw reclass ($18,750 to 211000); partner-signed May 2026.
- **FY2025 federal + state tax preparation** — Form 1065, partner K‑1s, M‑1 reconciliation, SALT (incl. a $4,820.30 closed-period SALT late-post); FY2025 Tax-Prep Data Package filed 2026‑06‑08; cycle re-kicked 2026‑06‑08.
- **Tax-planning advisory** — FY26 partner-cadence model projecting ~$180K aggregate tax savings (Schmidt retirement / Q4 cadence shift).

**Conclusion:** the accounting, annual-accounts, tax-compliance and advisory work has **no engagement letter, addendum, or change order on file.** We have effectively gone **out of documented scope** on everything except the FY2026 audit, and that expansion has **not been documented**. This directly contradicts the firm's own standing guidance on the recent multi-entity win ("keep first-phase scope limited to the signed engagement letters," "avoid any implied expansion of scope" — Steven Perry / Matthew Li, 2026‑05‑22).

**Independence note for the partner conversation:** Brookfield is simultaneously keeping Northstar's books, preparing its tax return, and (per the 2026‑05‑11 letter) performing the **FY2026 audit** of the same entity. The combination of bookkeeping + tax + audit for one client is a **self-review / independence threat** that should be flagged to Matthew alongside the scope/fee question. (We are also recording Brookfield's own audit fee inside Northstar's books: $35,000 FY2026 audit retainer received + $7,000 Q1 audit-fee accrual.)

---

## 5. Accounts Payable queue — Northstar vendor invoices stuck in queue

We process Northstar's AP. **Every one of the 87 invoices in SAP is `pending_approval` — none approved, paid, or voided.**

| Aging (by invoice date, as of 2026‑06‑12) | # | Value |
|---|---|---|
| 0–60 days | 5 | $13,886.89 |
| 61–90 days | 7 | $13,259.55 |
| 91–180 days | 25 | $97,514.95 |
| 181–365 days | 50 | $177,401.83 |
| **Total pending_approval** | **87** | **$302,063.22** |
| of which **> 90 days** | **75** | **$274,916.78** |

- By GL: 210000 (external vendors) 61 invoices / $220,855; 219000 (reimbursements) 26 invoices / $81,208.
- Oldest items run **325–339 days** (e.g. CrownPeak VEN‑030‑817856 $1,152.41 @ 339d; AdvoSphere VEN‑039‑15742 $5,147.22 @ 326d; LexHarbor VEN‑001‑551785 $9,863.97 @ 325d).
- Largest aged items not in any comms thread: VaultSpan VEN‑021‑709994 **$12,896.12** (250d), BlueMesa VEN‑018‑339868 **$10,998.09** (~324d), Stonepath VEN‑025‑944371 **$16,213.76** (137d), ArchiveSpring VEN‑033‑523270 **$11,786.59** (130d), Cobalt Harbor VEN‑036‑501397 **$10,866.53** (96d).

---

## 6. Do the comms match the numbers?

**No — there is a material gap.**
- **AP:** Slack/email/Linear only ever discuss **3 small CrownPeak invoices (~$1.8K total)** — VEN‑030‑353041 ($459.84, duplicate), VEN‑030‑736427 ($422.32, stale-void), VEN‑030‑817856 ($1,152.41, mis-coded 210000→219000). The actual backlog is **$302K across 87 invoices**, 75 of them >90 days. The narrative captures <1% of the exposure.
- **WIP:** Slack threads (persona_007/009/015) show the WIP recognition JE being held "pending billable-hours tie-out / client input," which is consistent with WIP not being cleanly relieved — but **no comms flag the $2.0M carried WIP or the missing WIP-relief mechanism.**
- **Scope:** Comms show the FY2025 annual accounts, FY2025 tax and FY26 advisory work all proceeding, but **no comms reference an engagement letter or change order covering that work** — confirming the documentation gap rather than resolving it.
- **Resolved vs. open:** the CrownPeak items are narratively "dispositioned" (partner-approved dismissals/void) but several remain `pending_approval` in SAP and open in Linear (see §7); they are *documented*, not *cleared*.

---

## 7. Open tracking items (not closed out)

**Linear (Northstar-labelled, still open):**
- `issue_0ab11dc6…` — Append VEN‑030‑736427 stale-void write-up — **in_progress**
- `issue_ba662bde…` — Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule — **in_progress**
- `issue_dfa400bc…` — Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification — **in_progress**
- `issue_05de9a8f…` — Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection — **todo**
- `issue_7e7797e4…` — Partner sign-off conditions on CrownPeak duplicate-detection — **todo**
- (`issue_70fdb3e2…` CrownPeak three-item arc — done)

**Airtable:** No dedicated Northstar engagement record existed. A new **AP Workflow Exceptions** record has been created to reflect engagement status (Northstar AP pending-approval backlog + scope/WIP/AR review pointer); the Northstar malpractice JE (JE‑northstar_legal‑FP‑2026‑05‑0002) is flagged "Escalated" in the Weekly Tax & Review Cadence tracker.

---

## 8. Recommendation — absorb vs. change order

The facts support **going to Matthew about a change order / engagement-letter addendum**, not quiet absorption, because:
1. The out-of-scope work (accounting, annual accounts, tax, advisory) is **substantial and recurring**, not a one-off April overage.
2. There is **no fee mechanism on file** for any of it — leaving it undocumented is both a revenue-leakage risk and a quality/independence-file gap.
3. The firm's own recent guidance is explicit about not letting scope expand without documentation, and Acme set the precedent (documented Addendum #1).

**Suggested next steps before/at the partner meeting:**
- Stand up an **engagement-letter addendum (or separate accounting/tax/advisory letter)** retro-covering the bookkeeping, annual-accounts, tax-compliance and advisory work, with fee basis; mirror the Acme Addendum #1 process.
- Pull a **true AR aging** and a **WIP-by-matter analysis with a billing/relief plan** so carried WIP is converted or written down.
- Get the partner to confirm the **independence position** on audit + bookkeeping + tax for the same client.
- Drive the **$302K / 87-invoice AP backlog** to disposition (approve / void / pay) — the 3 CrownPeak items are a rounding error against the real queue.

*Figures sourced from Oracle GL (northstar_legal), SAP sub-ledger AP, Records Vault, Linear, Airtable, Slack, email and internal messaging, as of 12 June 2026.*

## 1. Bottom line

The Northstar engagement looks "messy from a few angles" because three things are true at once:

1. **We are performing accounting, annual-accounts, tax-compliance and tax-advisory work for Northstar, but the only engagement letter on file in Records Vault is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026‑05‑11).** There is no bookkeeping / management-accounts / tax / advisory engagement letter or change-order/addendum on file. The expanded work is therefore **undocumented as to scope and fee** — the opposite of how the parallel Acme Cloud overage was handled (documented Engagement Letter Addendum #1, partner-countersigned).
2. **The WIP we are carrying does not tie out.** WIP (acct 119000) is **$2,013,942.79** and only ever grows — it is recognised straight to revenue and is **never relieved to AR/billings**. There is no WIP→invoice conversion happening.
3. **The AP queue we run for Northstar is badly backed up** — **all 87** vendor invoices sit in `pending_approval` (**$302,063.22**), 75 of them (**$274,917**) over 90 days, none approved or paid. The comms only ever discuss 3 small CrownPeak items (~$1.8K). The story in Slack/email does **not** match what the numbers show.

A scope conversation (absorb vs. change order) is warranted. Supporting detail below.

---

## 2. Accounts Receivable — outstanding, aging & stale risk

GL is Northstar's own ledger, which we maintain.

| Metric | Account | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| Accounts Receivable – Client Billings | 110000 | **$647,359.49** |
| Allowance for Doubtful Accounts | 117000 | **$1,057,727.96** (credit) |
| Bad Debt Expense (YTD) | 590000 | **$427,018.64** |
| Deferred Revenue – Client Retainers | 240000 | $2,043,052.32 |

**Stale-risk read:**
- The allowance ($1.06M) is **~163% of net AR** and bad-debt expense is **$427K YTD** — both abnormally high. In FP‑2026‑04 alone there were **~$49.9K of bad-debt write-offs** across five separate JEs (e.g. $16,033.85, $15,924.32, $8,421.99, $7,258.47, $2,274.39).
- This pattern — a reserve larger than the live ledger plus continuous monthly write-offs — is the signal that **aged receivables are routinely going uncollectible and being absorbed through bad debt** rather than collected. That is the "going stale" risk in concrete terms.
- Cash is still turning (client-payment JEs of $133.2K, $106.1K, $82.9K in the month), so this is a **collectibility / aged-bucket** problem, not a total-collection failure.
- *Limitation:* invoice-level aging is not exposed in the GL; the reserve and write-off behaviour is the available stale-risk evidence. Recommend a true AR aging be pulled from the billing sub-ledger before the partner meeting if Matthew wants bucketed figures.

| Account | Mar‑26 close | Apr‑26 close | Current |
|---|---|---|---|

- **The structural problem:** WIP recognition entries are booked **DR 119000 / CR Revenue** (401000/404000/406000). Billing entries are booked separately as **DR 110000 AR / CR Revenue** ("Litigation engagement revenue realization"). **Nothing ever credits 119000.** Across every period reviewed, WIP has *zero* credit activity.

Either way, **$2.0M of carried WIP is unsupported by a corresponding billing/relief mechanism.**

input.subject: Northstar engagement — full status review (scope / WIP / AR / AP) ahead of the Matthew meeting
input.content:
Daniel,

Per your ask this morning, here is where the Northstar engagement actually stands across the systems (Oracle GL, SAP AP, Records Vault, Linear, Airtable, Slack/email/messaging), as of 12 June 2026. Full memo with supporting detail is filed in Records Vault: "Northstar Legal — Engagement Status, Scope & Billing Review (2026-06-12)" (doc_2359e3ad6bc9, FIRM_INTERNAL / restricted). A tracking record is also open in Airtable (AP Workflow Exceptions, rec5c0e9f38545745).

BOTTOM LINE — three things are true at once, and they point to a change-order conversation with Matthew, not silent absorption:

1) SCOPE — we appear to be out of documented scope.
The ONLY engagement letter on file for Northstar is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11). There is NO bookkeeping / management-accounts / tax / advisory engagement letter, addendum or change order on file — yet we are doing all of it: monthly bookkeeping & close (since at least Jul-2025), management accounts (FP-2026-04 package), FY2025 annual accounts (partner-signed May-2026), FY2025 federal+state tax prep (Form 1065/K-1/M-1/SALT, incl. a $4,820.30 SALT late-post; cycle re-kicked 08-Jun), and tax-planning advisory (FY26 partner-cadence model, ~$180K projected savings). That expansion is undocumented as to scope and fee — the opposite of how we handled the Acme overage (documented Engagement Letter Addendum #1, partner-countersigned). Independence flag for Matthew: we are simultaneously keeping the books, preparing the tax return, AND (per the May letter) auditing the same entity — a self-review/independence threat. (We're also booking our own audit fee in their ledger: $35K retainer received + $7K Q1 accrual.)

2) WIP — does NOT tie out.
WIP (acct 119000) is $2,013,942.79 and only ever grows (Mar close $1.80M → Apr $1.91M → now $2.01M). WIP recognition is booked DR 119000 / CR revenue, and billing is booked separately as DR 110000 AR / CR revenue — nothing ever credits 119000. WIP is never relieved when work is billed, so the $2.0M carried balance does not tie to billings or to approved invoices. ~$69.1K was recognised in Apr (matches the BD1 note) plus $46.9K approved-but-not-posted in a now-closed period. This needs a WIP-by-matter clean-up and a billing/relief plan.

3) AR — real stale risk.
AR (110000) is $647,359.49, but the allowance (117000) is $1,057,727.96 — ~163% of net AR — and YTD bad-debt expense (590000) is $427,018.64, with ~$49.9K of write-offs in April alone. Cash is still turning (client payments of $133K/$106K/$83K in the month), so this is an aged-bucket / collectibility problem being absorbed through bad debt, not a total-collection failure. (Invoice-level aging isn't exposed in the GL; recommend pulling a true AR aging from the billing sub-ledger if Matthew wants bucketed numbers.)

AP — the queue we run for Northstar is badly backed up.
ALL 87 Northstar vendor invoices in SAP are pending_approval = $302,063.22; none approved, paid or voided. 75 of them ($274,917) are >90 days old; 50 ($177,402) are 181–365 days; oldest is 339 days (CrownPeak VEN-030-817856). Largest aged items: Stonepath $16,213 (137d), VaultSpan $12,896 (250d), ArchiveSpring $11,786 (130d), BlueMesa $10,998 (~324d), Cobalt Harbor $10,866 (96d).

COMMS vs NUMBERS — they do NOT match.
Slack/email/Linear only ever discuss 3 small CrownPeak invoices (~$1.8K total: VEN-030-353041 duplicate, VEN-030-736427 stale-void, VEN-030-817856 mis-coded). That narrative captures <1% of the $302K backlog and says nothing about the $2.0M WIP, the AR reserve, or the scope gap. The CrownPeak items are also "documented" not "cleared" — several remain pending_approval in SAP and the Linear follow-ups (issue_0ab11dc6, ba662bde, dfa400bc in_progress; 05de9a8f, 7e7797e4 todo) are still open.

RECOMMENDATION for the partner meeting:
- Take a change order / engagement-letter addendum to Matthew retro-covering the bookkeeping, annual-accounts, tax-compliance and advisory work, with a fee basis (mirror the Acme Addendum #1 process). The work is substantial and recurring, not a one-off — absorbing it quietly is both revenue leakage and a quality/independence-file gap.
- Confirm the partner's independence position on audit + bookkeeping + tax for the same client.
- Pull a true AR aging and a WIP-by-matter billing/relief plan.
- Drive the $302K / 87-invoice AP backlog to disposition; the 3 CrownPeak items are a rounding error against the real queue.

Happy to pull the per-invoice AP listing or the WIP-by-matter detail before you sit down with Matthew — just say the word.

George

content.text:
Done. Here's where the Northstar engagement actually stands and what I've put in place.

## What I found

**1. Scope vs. engagement letter — we're out of documented scope.**
The *only* engagement letter on file in Records Vault is the **FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter (executed 2026‑05‑11)**. Yet we're performing — with no bookkeeping/tax/advisory letter, addendum or change order on file — monthly bookkeeping & close, management accounts, FY2025 annual accounts (partner‑signed May), FY2025 federal+state tax prep (Form 1065/K‑1/M‑1/SALT), and tax‑planning advisory (~$180K savings model). That contradicts the firm's own standing guidance ("limit scope to signed engagement letters; avoid implied expansion"). There's also an **independence/self‑review flag**: we keep the books, prepare the return, *and* audit the same entity.

**2. WIP — does not tie out.** WIP (119000) is **$2,013,942.79** and only ever grows. WIP is recognised DR 119000 / CR revenue, billing is booked separately DR 110000 / CR revenue, and **nothing ever credits 119000** — so it's never relieved and doesn't tie to billings or approved invoices.

**3. AR — genuine stale risk.** AR (110000) **$647,359.49**, but allowance (117000) **$1,057,727.96** (~163% of net AR) and YTD bad debt **$427,018.64** (~$49.9K written off in April alone) — aged buckets being absorbed through bad debt.

**4. AP — badly backed up.** **All 87** Northstar vendor invoices are `pending_approval` = **$302,063.22**, none approved/paid; **75 ($274,917) over 90 days**, oldest 339 days.

**5. Comms ≠ numbers.** Slack/email/Linear only ever discuss 3 small CrownPeak invoices (~$1.8K, <1% of the backlog) — nothing on the $302K AP queue, $2.0M WIP, AR reserve or scope gap. CrownPeak items are "documented," not cleared (Linear issues still in_progress/todo).

## What I did
- **Records Vault:** filed the full memo with supporting detail — `doc_2359e3ad6bc9`, kind=memo, **classification: restricted**, **retention: FIRM_INTERNAL** (matching the engagement‑letter record), tied to `northstar_legal`.
- **Airtable:** created an engagement‑status record in *AP Workflow Exceptions* (`rec5c0e9f38545745`, owner George McAdam, severity High, Open) summarising the AP backlog + WIP/AR/scope findings.
- **Email:** sent the full briefing to you and Daniel (`120acdf6e55e40d78b61868b8257529f`) so he can walk into the Matthew meeting prepared.

**Recommendation in the memo:** take a change order / engagement‑letter addendum to Matthew (mirror the Acme Addendum #1 process) rather than absorb — the out‑of‑scope work is substantial and recurring, with no fee mechanism on file.

Two notes of caution: the GL figures are Northstar's *own* books (which we maintain), so invoice‑level AR aging isn't exposed there — I flagged that a true aging should be pulled from the billing sub‑ledger if Matthew wants bucketed numbers. I did not approve/pay/void any AP items or alter any JEs — those are dispositions for the partner conversation.
